final_answer: COVID-19 spreads primarily through the air via tiny droplets when people cough, sneeze, breathe, sing, or talk, and can also be transmitted by touching the face after contacting contaminated surfaces. The virus can spread from individuals who are asymptomatic, increasing the risk of transmission in close contact settings, especially with poor airflow. Living with someone who has COVID-19 or spending time in crowded spaces further elevates the risk of infection [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00000, doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00008, doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00007, doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00011, doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00010].
